name: Same-day emergency quote with a rush surcharge
description: Customer called about a burst pipe. User dictates a rush quote and the assistant should both flag urgency in the customer-facing copy and add a clear rush-fee line.

persona.name: Marcus Lee
persona.context: Phone rang at 7am. Wants a quote out before he drives over so the customer agrees in writing.

steps.action: Types: 'Emergency for the Diaz family — burst pipe, $250 labor, $80 parts, $100 same-day rush fee. Need to send right now.'
steps.expect: Assistant creates a quote with three lines totaling $430.00; summary mentions 'emergency' or 'same-day'
Quote locks; the email queued to the customer carries an 'urgent' tone in subject/preview without looking spammy

emails.subject: Same-day quote for your burst pipe — Marcus
emails.expect: Subject conveys urgency; body has a one-line description, total, and an Accept button visible without scrolling on mobile

evaluationCriteria.check: Customer email looks personal, not transactional — sender name is the contractor's, not 'no-reply'
User can go from voice/text to sent quote in under 60 seconds
Rush fee is a separate line, not folded into labor — easier to defend if disputed
Email passes basic alt-text and heading checks (e.g., Litmus alt-text spot check)
